---
title: "Configuration"
---

`oboro` reads the nearest `oboro.toml`, searching upwards from the working directory.
Without one it uses the defaults below.
Every section is optional.

```toml
# Region used to interpret national phone number formats.
default_region = "FR"

# The local recognition model.
ner_enabled = true
ner_threshold = 0.15

# Values that must never be redacted.
allowlist = ["My Own Company Ltd", "Lille"]

# Terms that must always be redacted.
[[denylist]]
term = "Acme Consulting SARL"
kind = "provider"

# Your own identifier formats.
[[patterns]]
name = "contract number"
regex = "CT-[0-9]{6}"
```

## `default_region`

A two-letter region code deciding how national phone numbers are read.
With `FR`, `06 12 34 56 78` is a valid mobile number.
With `GB` it is not, and would be left alone.

## `ner_enabled` and `ner_threshold`

`ner_enabled` turns the recognition model off without rebuilding.
`ner_threshold` is the probability a detection must reach before it is acted on.

Lower it to redact more, raise it to redact less.
The default of `0.15` errs towards redacting, for reasons set out in [Limitations](limitations.qmd).

## `allowlist`

Values that must never be redacted, whatever finds them.
Your own company name belongs here, and so does anything the model keeps misreading as a name.

Matching ignores case and surrounding whitespace, and understands accents: an entry of `Société Générale` matches `SOCIÉTÉ GÉNÉRALE`.

## `denylist`

Terms that must always be redacted, whether or not anything else spots them.
This is how you handle a client list you already know.

```toml
[[denylist]]
term = "Globex Industries"
kind = "company"
```

`kind` decides which placeholder the value gets, and defaults to `organisation`.
Recognised values are `person`, `organisation`, `address`, `phone` and `email`, along with a few synonyms such as `client`, `provider` and `company`.
Anything else becomes a placeholder of its own name.

Matching ignores case and respects word boundaries, so `Acme` matches `ACME` but not `acmentioned`.

## `patterns`

Your own identifier formats, as regular expressions.

```toml
[[patterns]]
name = "contract number"
regex = "CT-[0-9]{6}"
```

The name becomes the placeholder tag, so this one produces `[[CONTRACT_NUMBER_1]]`.
Patterns you declare are treated as the most specific kind there is, because you declared them deliberately.

::: {.callout-tip}

## Anchor your patterns

A pattern such as `[0-9]{6}` matches any six digits anywhere, including inside longer numbers.
Prefer something with a distinctive prefix, or anchor it with `\b`.

:::

The regular expression syntax is [Rust's `regex` crate](https://docs.rs/regex/latest/regex/#syntax), which has no backreferences and no lookaround.
An invalid expression is reported with the name of the pattern that contains it, rather than being ignored.
